# Magic Tournament Scheduler

> Round-robin tournament scheduler for Magic: The Gathering drafts (a pod of 1v1 matches). Everyone plays everyone exactly once; standings rank by wins with head-to-head and strength-of-victory tiebreakers.

Enter player names, generate a single round-robin schedule, record each best-of-3 match result, and watch standings update live. Odd player counts get one bye per round (each player byes exactly once); a bye counts as a 2–0 win. All state is in memory only — nothing is persisted, a refresh starts over.

## Routes
- `/` — the whole app. Two phases: Setup (enter players) → Running, which has two tabs: **Rounds** (pairings + result entry) and **Standings**.

## How it works
- **Scheduling:** circle method. N players → N−1 rounds (even) or N rounds (odd, via a phantom bye). Produces every C(N,2) pairing once.
- **Results:** per match, pick the winner and game score (2–0 / 2–1). Byes are auto-wins.
- **Standings sort:** (1) points = match wins; (2) head-to-head — resolved as a mini-league among only the players tied on points (handles 2-way and N-way ties); (3) **Beat-%** — average match-win% of the opponents you defeated (the user's "win % of the people you beat"). Name is the final stable tiebreak.
- **Extra columns:** GW% (game-win%), OMW% (classic opponents' match-win% over everyone faced, shown for reference, not used in sorting).

## Data API
- None. No backend; state lives in browser memory for the session only.

## Notes for agents
- Core logic is pure and isolated in `tournament.js` (`buildSchedule`, `computeStandings`, `sortStandings`) with no React/DOM deps — import it directly to test or reuse.
- No-build app: htm + esm.sh, no bundler. `index.html` is the entry.
